description_long: This object is a rectangular, vintage-style radio receiver with a predominantly orange body and black front panel. The front face features a row of blue-lit buttons or indicators, likely for tuning or preset selection, and a dark speaker grille area. The sides are solid orange, with one end displaying a prominent circular dial or knob, possibly for tuning or volume adjustment. The back is mostly flat orange with a recessed panel, likely for battery or power access, and a small metallic component. The overall design is compact and robust, with visible seams and hardware details suggesting a durable, utilitarian build. The radio's aesthetic and construction make it suitable for use in urban environments such as garages, workshops, or outdoor settings.
description: A compact, orange vintage radio receiver with a black front panel featuring blue-lit buttons and a prominent tuning knob on one side. Its robust, rectangular design is accented by a dark speaker grille and metallic details.
description_view_0: The front view shows the black panel with blue-lit buttons and a dark speaker grille, highlighting the main interface of the radio.
description_view_1: The left side view reveals the orange body and a large circular tuning knob, with minimal additional features visible.
description_view_2: The rear view displays a flat orange surface with a recessed panel and a small metallic component, likely for power or battery access.
description_view_3: The right side view shows a plain orange surface with a small raised detail, lacking the prominent knob seen on the opposite side.
